column25: I would like to suggest to first improve setting the right expectations and communication to the customer. All communication except from the side of the on-site project manager is a farce. I work for a Fortune 500 organization and run an operations team, hence I do appreciate some of the challenges but this is beyond my understanding that how the most relevant client management/service contacts fail to never circle back with any sort of updates or reasonable communication. I consider handing the responsibility of handing over the interior work of my home to Bonito as one of the worst decisions I have taken in life. I will definitely be voicing out my very disappointing experience with Bonito on a public platform very soon. I would like to thank Mr.Shijin for his excellent work during the project execution phase but the rest of the org. is just a circus and a joke.

column21: The whole thing started very well. We as customers were introduced to our designer Ananya and customer CRM Kiran. Since we lived in HSR we were looking for someone from the same branch but still were introduced to a designer who worked in some far away branch. We still went with that considering we had faith on Ananya. Till the design phase everything went well but after that Ananya messed up everything she missed telling us some very important flaws in her design because of which we had to change our MBR design and even the kitchen design had to be changed. We had explicitley told her we would need laminates which would not scratch easily but still the kind she gave had got scratches even before we moved in. Even the project manager told us we should not have taken these laminates since they scratch easily. The result is even before we have moved in our cupboard is full of scratches.  The worst behaviour was by the Project head Belinda. So we decided to go for loan after talking to Belinda, Kiran Ananya and everyone in the team. We decided to take a loan on the amount which was left after discount. But after 50% of the amount was payed Belinda called us to inform that they would have to withdraw the discount since its against there rules. From the very beginning the team knew that we were planning to take a loan to move fwd but to get the project they lured us with the discunt and when we had payed 50% of the amount they withdrew the discount. Through out the process Belinda was never available we had to call her several times to get a response that too not over call only messages. I am really really dissapointed to attach myself with Bonito. Very very unprofessional bunch of people.

column20: Better design, better communication, better customer handling, better timeline adhere, better clarity, better everything

Oh…!! You have lot to improve.  Almost all of the negative review that has been written about you is 100% true and this statement is made only after we have experienced it firsthand. If you have not attended/addressed those, it gives a clear understanding that you do not want to improve in any aspect and just sign the project, get 100% of the project value and leave the customer to suffer. We deeply regret that we have chosen Bonito as our interior partner.   Still, below are the few points if you want to address and improve –  1.	You do not have an escalation matrix. The escalation point stops at the Project Head level and when your project head is choosing to ignore the multiple communication you send them, it is very important for a customer that there should some grass to hold and sail. We really wonder how a Project Head can be so irresponsible, ignorant and  in spite of all this behavior, argue that they are right. 2.	The Senior and Top Management do not know what is happening at the execution level, How their customers is treated, is not a topic of interest for Bonito Management. I had requested many times to escalate my mail to senior management or at least to next level, nobody is bothered and in the meantime the issue is also not addressed. My belief is that, the traits in any corporate organization will get cascade from top. So it is very clear that the top management have given them their full support & authority to behave the way they are behaving. I have been consumer, customer and even vendor at various stage of by life. At least I have not seen anyone treating the customer the way Bonito is treating their customer (I have personal feedback from more than 4 customers too) right on their face .  If Top management really have some concern about their organization , kindly go through the series of mails I had written to Bonito during the execution Phase.  3.	On “How was the quality of material and workmanship” –  Ours is a very infamous project in Bonito. Multiple units of our project has gone 2-3 time for rectification because of issues caused by design, dimension, quality, etc.  One month of usage, we already experience the air bubbles in the acrylic, breakage of lift up mechanism.  I would rate 5 for the Project Managers dedication and support for this project closure. Rest everything is rated at 1.

The project designs are average, and the Project team of Dhiraj L, Ruchi Chaturvedi, and Vaishali Salve failed to add professional-level value to the projects.  Vaishali Salve, the Project head, has never initiated any communication; I had to follow up and get the answers for every piece of information.  And Vaishali moved the project to production when the design was still being finalized, which led to a lot of design compromise.  Dhiraj falsely promised many things and had yet to deliver his commitments.  I had to escalate multiple times to get the project to this state.  George, the salesperson, only sold the idea of a designer home and never found him afterward.  Suchandra, the project manager, and Mohsin khan, Operations Manager, intervened and helped complete the project.

At Ground level, carpenter are not at all profession unlike how Bonito has been throughout the phases. They work in a hurry to finish of given task. They don't cut the ply pieces as per the wall. There is always gap and can see the cut is not straight. Also when they cut a piece of ply they are not applying edge band which is provided by bonito. The ply is completely exposed at one end. Due to their carelessness, there was alignment issue in kitchen, broken one kitchen tile, few more small alignment issue in other room. Now regarding deep cleaning, the cleaning was not at all satisfactory for the amount you charge. I had to spend 5k again for deep cleaning which was better. That was complete waste of 12 14k just to remove laminate sheet, as floor cleaning was done  just for the sake of doing.. corners under sink cabinet all that was not cleaned properly. Photos won't show the work what they have done. Special thanks to Dhamo for the design cordination and everything and Yogesh, who was always reachable and tried to get the things done in proper manner. The reputation what bonito is building throughout all the phases, is clearly getting spoiled as the ground level due to untrained/careless/unprofessional carpenters and cleaners. That's the reason I have given 7 (wanted to give 6). As the main phase - execution phase, if that's done proper then there is no use of all the designing planning manufacturing done with so much precision.

I had a terrible experience working with Bonito rights from the the design, execution and overall completion. 1) Designer missed on a lot of important aspects, changed a few things behind the scenes without informing customer and did not create the design as per the site, wherein we had to intervene at a lot of times to get it corrected. Also, there were quite a few things changes post 3D sign-off by us in the 2D design which were not informed and it was directly executed on site. The designer did not care to visit the site even once during execution and was very later in responding to any queries. 2) Project head only reached out to us for payment and once all the advance payment was done she was least aware and interested in the timely execution and delivery. We had to call her multiple times through the past 6 months to even get some basic response. Overall, project has been delayed by around 5 months (initial completion date was August 8, 2022).  2) Bonito as a whole need to be a lot more professional and customer friendly which was not at all the case with us. We had to invest a lot of personal time and energy on things which ideally Bonito should have taken care of. Really bad experience working with Bonito and I would never recommend working with Bonito for any of my friends or family. The least we expect now from Bonito is delayed compensation would be done rightly and services provided as per warranty commitments.
We expected better response whenever we had any concern

Be transparent, respond to customer and develop some quality standards in your firm.  Learn to collaborate internally and coordinate with customer instead of being haughty and doing whatever you feel like doing and messing with customers. Overall your designers have no imagination or competence. I think you should just focus on building modular boxes instead of calling yourself a design firm

This grooving was supposed to be done at the beginning of the project itself. Am sorry for this long explanation but had to vent these out gracefully so that other customer won’t undergo same pain.

However, for me, the experience has been rather quite disappointing.  Apathy from the Design and Execution Team: Though your salespersons get time to call me a dozen of times while making a sale, but the situation takes a U turn when it comes to provide service. It is I who have to follow continuously to get updates. Most of the times, my phone is not attended. To add to my surprise, false promises and blatant lies were thrown at me!  Negligence: I was confirmed that wrong materials were ordered for me! I just wonder how can this obvious mistake be committed by anyone where technology handles everything else.  In a nutshell, the journey for me with Bonito Designs has been horrible. There are Design mismatches after execution, no quality finish. I hope you would take cognizance of this matter for greater good for your other clients who have come believing in Bonito.

We lost the count of the time designers were changed in our project, we had to restart from square 1 as they have no clue to what we want. This results in delay of designing and finalization. There was hardly any communication from your end. From designing to execution, we had to ask for updates and work instead of the team informing us. There were even occasions when the messages and calls were completely ignored or not even seen,  multiple times. Eveb when you change the designer we get to know when we call them for uodages and they be like, ive already left the company. The overall experience with Bonito Designs has been very poor. I will split the feedback for each phase:  1. Design Phase - Design phase was good. The designer Neha understood the requirements and could create a good design. 2. Commercials - There were major issues with pricing process. There were errors in the tool. The prices that were shared during the design presentation got changed. Even after signing off on the final commercial I was told that there were errors in laminate pricing. I had to follow up a lot to get the issues fixed. It meant wasting quite a few days to get the commercial fixed. 3. Production - I am not at all happy with the quality of work and the final workmanship. Let me share some instances. a) 3 shutters of one of the cabinet were damaged. Bonito tried to paint the damaged area with white paint and pass it on. It was only when I escalated it was agreed to change. The change took more than 10 days thereby impacting the schedule. b) The puja unit had gaps and eventually had to fill the gaps using silicon. c) The team opened the basin pipes at the time of fitting the bathroom storage but never bothered to fix the same. They neither asked me before opening the pipes nor informed me that they have left it like that. d) There are gaps in the doors of all the cabinets. The quality of fitting is pathetic.

Designer Santosh is amazing, Design was really good and elegant,  but it should have been more specific regarding what can not be done by Bonito at all. and It should have been more clear the hardware provided by Bonito. Cross measurement should also consider wall angles and design accordingly. Design time QC: I have no clue, about their role, they did not add much value.  Project Manager: Project manager Mawia is an amazing co-ordinator, Mawia was very responsible and He made sure that we were satisfied with all the work. and also he was accurate in getting the work delivered as per the designed documents. Good experience to work with a professional supervisor to this project.  Quality of work and quality of hardware and wood are outstanding. It has made our house beautiful.  QC after execution: QC was appreciated, as he could find out the slightest of the mistakes and get them corrected immediately.  Labours (Carpenter, Duko Pianter and Cleaners) were the best at their loyal work.  No need to introduce Design manager, QC (design time) manager to the clients, they have no interaction with customer, and they will not respond to the call/messages.
Better co-ordination with the on-site worksman and client

There are lots of things that you can do better. We had the worst of experiences possible with Bonito and regretting our decision to hire them. First of all, the cost is very high if you check your competitors in the market but we decided to extend our budget in the hopes that the work done will be the best and also we won't need to be present or overlooking the work as we were not staying in Bangalore at the time. Design phase - There were so many discussions around every single thing and our designer Jasmita will just listen to us and incorporate something which will mess up 10 other things e.g. Kitchen cabinets on top and bottom were not aligned and not of same size because we wanted something else. Instead of guiding us, we ended up guiding her and finding designs for her. One of those after effect is our chimney aluminium pipe is going to be visible and the answer was - that this was shown in 3d sign off and we wanted certain cabinets to be certain size and hence this happened. I would expect my designer to give me intimation of what the result is looking like. I am not sure what do you call yourselves but your job does not end at painting a good picture in 3d sign off and then ending up having wires, pipes everywhere. I am sorry but those are not so called international standards. If you call yourself big, you should show real house pictures to customers not 3d sign offs because you clearly are not trying to make the house look like that. Another issue was she used a french blue colour for duco kitchen design but when we cam to finalise colors , no pastel color were available which was ver sad as that was our number one priority and suddenly after paying booking amount we get to know there is not much we can do here. I also don't think the designer has utilised the space to maximum efficiency. I particularly asked for a matte duco but that was never mentioned in document and hence we had glossy paint and we caught it in time and got the second coating matte. the metal partition was supposed to be of sofa's arm length but the design we chose extended that and no one thought of mentioning that to us except providing length in 3d sign off. Are all your customers very handy with measurements or are you supposed to do that job. For the Project head - She never was aware of any progress and only talked to us when payment was due. She made sure everything was paid on time and warned us of late charges every single time even when we were moving from London to India over a weekend. But she did not manage to get the project done at time. Such a disaster!! is it all transactional for you guys? and if that is what it is, why bother making homes for people. Project manager - I believe our project manager tried a lot to finish on time but never got the hold of vendors or the material to be provided from Bonito. I don't know how PMs work in Bonito but he was definitely on receiving end of our frustrations but I felt he was helpless himself so there is a major structural issue here and Operations manager only got involved towards the end but same story as PM. Everything will be done tomorrow but that day never came. We had to be present in person last 2 weeks to get work done and after endless emails/messages/requests we were able to finish 9pm on a friday because we were flying out saturday morning and taking keys with us. I have more and more feedback for you guys with all details. so If anyone wants to get in touch and I hope someone does, then please call or meet.
Pls have the live status update mechanism and real escalation matrix, customer struggle throughout the process

column25: 1) Extremely disappointed with the lack of customization options. Customers have to go with what Bonito has available else have to hear No. Need to provide more customization options. It cannot be take whatever Bonito has. 2) Extremely disappointed with the lack of Design ideas from design team. We were looking for a guide who could help us customize the design as per our requirement. Instead, we had to do endless hours of R&D ourselves to get what we want within Bonito set of pallete. When asked for suggestions, we got such suggestions from design team which clearly showed lack of significant practical experience on ground. Please give Fresh & Practical Design ideas and do not put that work on customer.  3) Had to descope entire initial scope from Bonito apart from MBR due to lack of customization / fresh design ideas. Please listen to the customer and attend to the requirements.  4) MBR Wardrobe was promised with a customized door during booking the service and it was said Bonito will help. During execution, we were told no help will be provided on the door, and it was put on us to fix without any help from Bonito. So, i got a wardrobe without a door.  Also 4 inches space has been wasted behind the wardrobe across 7 feet width, so space management is also big disappointment here. Please do not go back on your word like this. Till date I did not get any further proactive help from Bonito on the same. 5) Dresser mirror design was shown edge-to-edge during 3D approval and actual Dresser Window provided is ~3/4th size. Same should have been communicated during 3D design and we could have chosen another option. Please stick to the approved design vs actual measurements and if any discrepancy consult the customer. 6) It was a single room in Bonito scope and timeline promised was 1 month. It took 2 months to finish in actual due to delay in replacement items dispatch and reaching the site in Mumbai from Bangalore. Please fix your Supply Chain so that customer does not suffer undue delay which affects his subsequent plans. 7) For any escalations, had to follow up multiple times to get Leadership attention. Please improve your leadership response time to customer issues. 8) Keeping aside the Design related disappointments - we specifically wanted to put out a good word for Sarvesh Chodankar (project manager) who has really done a great job on execution. Thanks Sarvesh!!

Overall rating: 7 / 10 Interior designing phase rating (when Rajini was incharge): 5 / 10 Execution phase rating (when Anand was in charge): 10 / 10  Here is the list of things which went well: 1. Interior designing was good and Rajini took our input from the start and suggested things accordingly 2. Quality of material used during interior is good and I am happy with final output 3. Execution of the design was very good and Anand did it seamlessly and things got completed on time.  Here is the list of things which didn't went well: 1. Even though I gave initial budget of 15 lacs including furniture and appliances to Bonito, I ended up spending 15 lacs without furniture and appliances. My budget has shot up by 30%. I specified my requirements from the start only. If it was not possible to do things within my budget then I should have been informed at the start.  2. There were lot of hidden costs which came up during design process which were not communicated to me at 10% payment phase. Some of these were: lighting in false ceiling, handles in all furniture, installation of extra switches for lights, granite installation, etc. These things resulted in me losing trust on Bonito which I think is most important for any business deal.  3. Customer support on Bonito side was very bad during design phase for all money related issues. Whenever we asked to see if some cost can be reduced or do something about hidden costs then both Naufil and Rajini didn't behave in the way business should behave with customers. They were very rude and behaved as if these things happen everywhere and we are idiots to raise this. How can anyone know that in a 1 lac costing wardrobe even handles will be extra?  4. Even after the final interior design was done there were some changes made during visit to apartment based on dimensions and other things. These could have been done during initial visit to apartment itself so that we do the design phase in one iteration and not over multiple. Some of these changes were: lighting not possible at multiple places due to chipping not being allowed, granite from builder can't be used, shifting of wardrobe due to fire sprinkler, change in handles of kitchen cabinet due to some dimension problem.  Overall I think you guys are doing good design and execution but overall process and hidden costs resulted in very bad customer experience which could be improved. I came to Bonito for great design and customer experience but could get only one of those. So overall I was disappointed.

column20: The whole thing started very well. We as customers were introduced to our designer Ananya and customer CRM Kiran. Since we lived in HSR we were looking for someone from the same branch but still were introduced to a designer who worked in some far away branch. We still went with that considering we had faith on Ananya. Till the design phase everything went well but after that Ananya messed up everything she missed telling us some very important flaws in her design because of which we had to change our MBR design and even the kitchen design had to be changed. We had explicitley told her we would need laminates which would not scratch easily but still the kind she gave had got scratches even before we moved in. Even the project manager told us we should not have taken these laminates since they scratch easily. The result is even before we have moved in our cupboard is full of scratches.  The worst behaviour was by the Project head Belinda. So we decided to go for loan after talking to Belinda, Kiran Ananya and everyone in the team. We decided to take a loan on the amount which was left after discount. But after 50% of the amount was payed Belinda called us to inform that they would have to withdraw the discount since its against there rules. From the very beginning the team knew that we were planning to take a loan to move fwd but to get the project they lured us with the discunt and when we had payed 50% of the amount they withdrew the discount. Through out the process Belinda was never available we had to call her several times to get a response that too not over call only messages. I am really really dissappointed to attach myself with Bonito. Very very unprofessional bunch of people.

column21: Initially, our interaction with Bonito presented a positive image of a professional and process-driven company. However, the reality started to unfold after the project initiation payment, where we paid 10% of the total project amount. Unfortunately, the professionalism we initially witnessed vanished once the payment was made.  All individuals who were actively involved during the design meeting, including Vrudhi (Sales), Pallavi (Designer Head), Aryaman Vyas (CRM), Shweta (Designer), and Chetana B (CRM), seemed to turn a blind eye as soon as the agreement with Bonito was signed.  Regrettably, due to the unprofessional conduct displayed by Bonito, we have decided to terminate our association with them. It took eight long months to finally get in touch with Bonito executives, and even then, they agreed to provide loose furniture only after deducting a penalty of 25K from the initial 10% amount.  To add to our disappointment, the product we eventually received from Bonito was not their own, but sourced from "City Furnish," a business known for furniture rentals. This lack of transparency in communicating such vital details during our discussions raises serious concerns about Bonito's business practices.

column25: Interior Design Project Feedback  I would like to share my experience following the completion of the interior work:  1. Design Process      Despite the relatively small scope of work, a significant amount of effort was required from my side to guide the design direction. Ideally, the design company should take greater ownership in understanding requirements and proactively proposing solutions.  2. Material Choices & Customization      The range of materials and customization options offered was quite limited, which restricted flexibility in achieving the desired outcome.  3. Payment vs. Flexibility      While payments are strictly enforced as milestones before progress, customer-specific requests are often declined citing company policies and processes. This creates an imbalance between financial commitment and service flexibility.  4. Need for Constant Follow-Up      Progress heavily depended on continuous follow-ups from my side. Without consistent reminders, work did not move forward as expected.  5. Post-Production Communication      After the production and execution phase, communication from the designer dropped significantly. Calls and messages were not responded to in a timely manner, which is concerning for post-installation support.  6. Render Accuracy      The design renders shared during the planning phase were not realistic. There was a noticeable gap between what was presented and the final execution, leading to unmet expectations.  7. Execution Quality      The quality of execution during the first installation was below expectations and requires improvement in terms of finishing and attention to detail.  ---  Overall Suggestion:   Improvement is needed in design ownership, communication, execution quality, and customer-centric flexibility to enhance the overall experience.

Please have Project Head or operations manager who take ownership and give updates and take constant feedback. We chose Bonito with the expectation that, given our clear vision for a basic modular design, everything would proceed smoothly without the need for constant oversight. We believed we wouldn't have to worry about the process, trusting Bonito to handle everything with great transparency. However, we must acknowledge that Bonito's sales team is excellent and a valuable asset to the company.  Unfortunately, Bonito needs to hire better designers and managers to uphold its brand. A manager promised us a follow-up call and email with an additional discount on Bonito's website for purchasing appliances, but we never heard back. After waiting and following up for two months, we finally received access but without any discounts.  The design, which I provided as a customer, took four months to finalize, despite my constant availability and missing work to provide input. The design team offered no input and failed to deliver the "celebrity design" service we paid for, which was falsely advertised. No complimentary offers or products were given, and there were no positive inputs or checks from the designer, management, or QC team when sharing estimates.  All we heard were reasons why things couldn't be done or were unavailable at Bonito. We repeatedly had to remind them of our requirements, which were often missed from the design despite detailed pointers. After four months, the design issues were finally addressed, but the quotation estimates contained errors and inflated prices, which we had to identify and correct ourselves.  The final execution at the site lacked clean finishes and proper alignments. Despite our observations, Bonito's team claimed these issues were technically impossible to fix. We have seen better finishes in carpenter-made houses without visible screws or joints, which Bonito couldn't provide despite being more expensive.  We do not recommend Bonito as we did not get what we wanted. For a basic modular setup, it took Bonito six months to complete with numerous design and management issues. Highly disappointed!

column21: Our experience with bonito has been mixed. We would like to share the detailed feedback below on all the categories:   Sales : Saad - rating 4/5  Saad shared the initial presentation to us and also explained the entire process and timelines with bonito. Our experience with him has been thoughtful and productive. He has been cooperative with us.   Design : Roja - 1/5  The worst phase and experience we had with bonito is for design phase. Below are few concerns with our designer Roja and her lead Smitha ( so overall design team )   - Roja is not skilled designer. She did not share any significant design inputs, which could have helped us. We had shared all the thoughts and kind of design we wanted for entrance foyer, tv unit, kitchen , wardrobes, bedroom wall, crockery unit and even study storage.   - Roja does not have basic sense of height ratio understanding. For example- how much height should be for a study table if that is made for a kid and adult of height 5’2’’. I think a designer must consider these aspects while designing a house. I have a spinal postural issue, which even she is aware of but she designed my study table of a height that I am not able to use it as it’s causing incorrect posture for back, neck and arms.   - we had shared the links of our electronic appliances, even then she wrongly estimated the height of our kitchen counter top which was supposed to cover the dishwasher and washing machine. We caught that height issue at the very last moment. She changed it but did not propose any design input for a space which could easily be converted into a pull out tray drawer. She increased the height of counter top and made the space dead by simply blocking it.   - she did not mention any L clamps in the puja unit tray. Which eventually the carpenter added.   - she could not prepare a design for bedroom wall ( behind bed) and made several random suggestions which were not aligning with our choices. At the end we made it on our own and got it done from a separate vendor   - we had asked for an arch border at the entrance foyer. She said that it can’t be done with putty and can only be done with a wood panel which she designed and created an estimation of 68k. Considering the exceptionally high estimate we removed that part from bonito’s scope and again got it done by ourself. To be precise, that arch design was easily done with mdf sheet cut and coated ya only 4K for foyer and 2 bedrooms altogether.   - Roja does not have sense on colour combinations as well. All those things were also shared by us for the design.   - she never created any 3D version of the design in front of us so I am not certain if she was actually designing or was just delegating it to someone else.  These are heavy gaps in designers skill set.  She is extremely unprofessional and never responded for our calls or messages on time. Their escalation matrix does not work at all. Her lead was Smitha and was also part of the what’s app group they created for our project. We rarely got any response or action from Smitha on Roja. Smitha had no control over designer and despite smiths being a design lead, there were 0 inputs from her side. Both have been very unprofessional and made our entire process extremely unpleasant with bonito.   Finance : Akshay- 3/5  Akshay is on point only for things related to finance. He will follow up and ensure that client is making all the payments, on an urgent basis and will keep reminding that any delays in payment will impact in project delays. He has no command over any other department, neither design, production nor execution.   Project Manager : Nandha, Deepak and Sachin. So we got 3 back to back PMs for our project. We were not notified for any of those changes done from bonito’s side. Nandha came over for only 1 discussion. There was no handover done from designer to Nandha. And Nandha did not give any handover to Deepak.  Deepak although have been a good experience. He was willing to help and tried a lot for smooth coordination. Be he lacked in accountability. He could not align carpenter, electrician, painter and cleaners. There were many gaps and delays because of that.   Sachin had joined at the very end but he is the only one who actually stood by his words and got the whatsoever remaining work done on time. He has been good in terms of all the communications and commitments. Hope he would have been the PM from day one, May be our experience would have been little better.    Carpenter: Jitender 5/5  He is the only person who did his job to extreme dedication and before the dedicated timeline. He is skilled for the job and have been a great experience for us.    Electrician: 1/5  Again an unskilled person was assigned to us. He blocked one of our primary power socket point in the kitchen, without having any discussion with us. He did the wrong wire connectivity for our puja unit as well.   Overall experience is 2/5.  By the end of project, we were only hoping that it should end quickly so that we can get some peace. That should never happen if we are paying so much amount.  Overall all the team members were very irresponsive for calls and messages. They never pick or reply to calls. Not respond to messages or emails. They even threatened us that our warranty will be void as we have got painting work done prior to the handover.  The hype over the brand is not worthy of the customer service. They should focus most on customer relation building and satisfaction over brand image building and advertising.

column25: Here is our feedback on the recent project 11684 your company undertook for us. While there were both positive and negative aspects, I believe addressing these can help improve future projects. First and foremost, I would like to commend your execution team. Their professionalism, attention to detail, and dedication were truly outstanding. Especially the Project manager Gokulnath was always reachable and responsive and provided us a positive experience with the organization which was not there otherwise. He and his team flawlessly executed ‘WHATEVER’ was designed and managed to stay on schedule despite major challenge with the kitchen countertop installation. Their hard work and commitment significantly contributed to the overall success of the project, and we for which are very pleased with the final results. However, I must express some concerns regarding the performance and promises made by the sales team. During our initial discussions, the expectations set were very high, promising an upmarket experience. Unfortunately, this did not fully translate into reality after the contract was signed.  Getting to more serious items below. Specifically, the design team encountered significant issues. There were challenges in understanding our requirements, particularly regarding the measurements of selected appliances for the cabinets. This often resulted in cabinets falling short in size, which was a recurring problem. Additionally, there were numerous communication challenges, including delays in responses and a lack of clarity in conveying design changes and updates. This led to frustration and a sense of disconnect during the planning phase. THE major concern was the kitchen countertop. During the design phase, it was discussed and shown in design images that a full-size granite slab [Though with 50% of wastage!!] would be provided for the island. However, post-production, we were informed that this was not possible, and a compromise was suggested that would have resulted in an island countertop with unsightly joints. To avoid this, we decided to change the material from granite to quartz, incurring additional costs. Despite this quick decision, and repeated follow up with Project head, there was a four-week delay for execution approval of the change, which was highly inconvenient. This is the most low rated item in the overall project. We shared all appliances [Washer Dryer, Dishwasher, OTG] make and model in advance with design team for making respective cabinets [screenshot of the conversation can be shared in email we will send soon].  After installation of the washing machine; 2 inches at the front of the appliance stands OUT of the cabinet!!  Another niggle is with the shutter panel / door of integrated dishwasher! To seat the DW within the cabinet; the back support has to be removed. Regarding front panel / shutter, once dishwasher is installed and shutter [in current size] is fitted; door will not open which need to fixed as in current scope.  OTG will project outside unless the back panel is removed!  I urge you to fix above appliance cabinet issues to our satisfaction with no additional cost. Team on ground are absolutely helpless with patchy components provided to them to execute.  Improving the communication channels and ensuring more timely and transparent updates would greatly enhance the overall experience. It would also be beneficial if the design team could place greater emphasis on understanding and accommodating specific requirements, such as appliance measurements, large / Island kitchen countertop measurements and materials to be used, their availability, final look and feel in such cases to avoid similar issues in the future.  Overall, while the end result met our expectations [once the couple of open issues are fixed timely], addressing the communication, requirement gathering and design recommendations, and measurement issues within the design team will significantly improve the customer experience. Once again we appreciate the hard work of your execution team and hope these insights are helpful for future projects.  Thank you for your attention to this matter.

column25: Communication needs to improve massively. We cannot be waiting a day almost for any response during project execution. Even when we communicate a day before, we are not getting responses in time and that's a massive turnoff .

Bonito - feedback & rating  The overall experience with Bonito is fine. We feel Bonito to get better customer satisfaction few things need to change. We have mentioned these based on pure our experience with Bonito from start (getting sales call) till handover.  Sales/Marketing Need to be little transparent. We felt more focus was given to get the client onboarded asap. Some clarifications should be provided at the beginning. * The sample design shown to onboard the client - it should be upfront mentioned that basic materials use, simple lay outs used for wardrobes etc. Otherwise, it comes a very higher side when final design is done at the latter stage, and we come to know this fact. Of course, it was mentioned the final cost will be more than 20%, but it easily comes more than that. For us it was 50% more. If we would have known this, initially we would have selected the range under 10L. * Clearly mention what do you mean by celebrity charge and how we will relate in the final product/outcome? We really don't know or understood where is Gauri khan or Manish Malhotra came into picture in whole of this process or what is their association really? Why unnecessarily we pay for that. Basically, need to justify ourselves it is celebrity design. In one case you say we can customise and then if you say since user has customized, no role of those celebrity designs. Then ok, but when then celebrity charge. This is we feel a very grey area which Bonito should take a note of it. Just know if you will be clearer and more transparent, more clients will automatically onboard. * Pricing structure should be explained upfront clearly explaining all breakups etc. In the first meeting itself you should clearly mention about the GST.  * The other thing we noticed more interests were shown to get contact numbers for any potential clients. I think the more focus should be given to the client onboarded first, provide all clarity upfront. Please understand that if the client knows everything about the interior etc why he/she has approached the company.   Design Phase Again, the overall experience was good. However, few things we will definitely want to call out (after we finally got the execution phase over) * What is not feasible should be assessed upfront rather finding that at very late post site visit and then making client hurry to take the call and pay the amount to move to next phase. We understand few things like actual measurements etc can vary. But if it is a complete unit/ledge etc not feasible as per the Bonito quality team's feedback, better to involve the feasibility team to take a look the design and give the fist hand feedback. It will help the client to think some alternatives well in advance or at least will be better informed. For our case it was a couple of diagonal ledges. I believe it could have easily checked and reverted back saying not possible. * Few minor things which will be extremely difficult for client/regular folks to find in the design docket which we felt should have informed from Bonito side itself. Here I am referring to the kitchen bottom cabinet (gas cylinder unit). We have told that the cylinder will be kept outside. But still as per standard design Bonito had to give us provision for gas cylinder unit, which is fine. However, we did not notice that in the bottom there will be two small patches of different colour. That is because the unit is a different box, and it does not match with the laminate we selected. We came to know this when the final execution happened. One can easily see why those two small portions looks different. When we asked this, we were told it is as per design and shown us the design. Then we came to know yes it was there but believe me it is such instance no one can see or catch in the design pdf. This would have been better if we were informed this will come like this finally. * Couple of such instances are TV unit top rack. It is 8 fit long and we got 6 ft and 2 ft ply board. This looks little odd as one can easily see there is a joint of two ply board. When we asked, we were told that is the standard size. This should have been informed earlier during design phase itself. If we consider this, then how the bottom ply board at the same TV unit does not have any joints? Similarly, Bonito should upfront inform where factory finishing can't be done, and carpentry is the only option. Whatever reasons we got for this (for our living hall partition) I am sure could be known earlier during the design phase. Then we could have selected some other designs where factory finishing would have given us better look and feel. Also, when suggested carpentry (at a very latter stage), we were not told it won't be as finishing as factory, the edges won't be that smooth etc.  * The other issue which we felt is lotsize of material. If client has asked some design and it requires material where there will be lot of wastage due to lotsize (as you have for whatever be the reason), better to inform upfront. The client can think alternatives or better go with informed what he or she has chosen. When someone goes to a designer company, we have put complete belief, but these things cause little dissatisfaction at then end.  * The client should be informed with pros/cons of any designs they come up in terms of material wastages, durability, feasibility, service issues etc to take the final call with more informed knowledge.  * Last but not the least. We feel client should be shown all the materials (which shown to some extent) and fittings like locks, knobs, handles, wall biddings etc upfront to get the feeling what are the things going to be used. We have provided MBR wardrobe handles because the design we selected required those which Bonito does not provide. Similarly, other things specially knobs, locks etc if client does not like what Bonito provides, should be given an option to own those as client's scope.  Execution Overall, it was fine with having regular updates. We haven't faced any noticiable issues to call out for. Overall Project management It was ok. What we felt it was more to make client hurry to get the payment.

Never faced something as painful as bonito’s client servicing. We have regretted putting our hard earned money every second. Besides Rachita, mohsin, Rohan, Afzal also failed us multiple times leaving us helpless. The communication lag, unprofessional work, careless attitude and absolutely no decision making attitude has led not only us down but also Bonito. Being a established company you have a lot of introspection to do and your way of working is just pathetic. Abhishek, who took charge of everything at very end was the only one who took the project seriously and made completed the work atleast. Overall experience with Bonito had been one of the most disappointing experience. To destroy someone’s first home like this, you guys should rethink your existence. Thank you but no thank you.

Dissatisfied Customer Satisfaction Survey, Awful Services, overcommitment and underperformance, worthless staff members excluding the Designer It was an awful experience and the decision I would regret to choose Bonito as our designing partner. None of the services and staff members were worth spending lakhs of rupees excluding your Designer Dhaval Chaudhari.  Details are mentioned as below. Kick off Meeting: It happened in Aug’23 and we got commitment of 90 days delivery promise with end-to-end services. Neither we got the delivery in 90 days, nor it was an end-to-end service. Designing based on the floor plan: Dhaval did a brilliant job of converting our interior needs to a 3D docket after listening very carefully. Measurement and cross measurements: Another false promise got exposed around 2 months after the sales meeting. As per the sales meeting the sales employee had an arrangement to do the measurement and cross measurement well before the possession of our new flat. It was a genuine lie to just win the business. Measurement and cross measurements happened only after the date of our flat possession. Operations: The first project manager conveyed that Bonito scope is very small in my property. He and his team can achieve the initially agreed date of final delivery as 16-Dec-23 well within the time if the keys are handed over for execution of work on or before 01-Dec-2023. We got our primary vendor work done in a span of 1 month. Please note the vendor’s scope was 5 times more than that of Bonito’s scope, still, he delivered his initial commitment. Bonito took the keys, and it looked like they disappeared for next two weeks. No responses to calls, messages and no proactive communication on progress of the work. This resulted in FULLY DISSAPPOINTED Customer like me! Furniture Alignment - There was no space for water pipe in filter area, no space for kitchen gas cylinder pipeline and what’s more the spice rack and dish trays were colliding with each other while operating them, such a SHAMEFUL Alignment! To top up the agony, CNC Assembly chosen in living room unit took like AGES to be installed.  QC- Looked like we did the QC before Bonito team did their job and sent the videos to project manager. I am not sure how serious the QC team is about Bonito’s delivery. Feedback on employees with whom I interacted. Zain Narveel:  He was a salesperson allotted to me who is an epitome of making false promises as below. A.	Commitment: I will personally be available for all your queries till end of the project  Reality: he disappeared soon after my first payment, never responded even to a general query B.	Commitment: Don’t worry on measurement and cross measurements, I will get this done for you much before your actual possession. Reality: He might not even know when that has taken place. C.	Commitment: I will help you in negotiation for the vendor work, loose furniture, and any other more specific needs in your project. Reality: He has absolutely no clue form where I got all these works completed D.	Commitment:  You are my brother, and I will come to have lunch at the place designed by Bonito just for you Reality: He was addressing me as Dhaval and his designer as Mandar, look at the level of absent mindedness in front of your client, you don’t even know the name of Bonito employee and that of a client. In general, Zain did the job of trapping me with range of low-line sales gimmick. I knew this from the time of sales meeting; however, I went with the brand and not with the underqualified sales skill of Mr. Zain. Udit Parmar: He was the Project Head for my flat who is a mixture of arrogance with senseless way of communication. One should train him on “Client Focus” competency over his extra vigilance of SOPs that needs to be revamped at Bonito’s level. Examples of how a project head should not talk to Client are mentioned below. a.	I will not penalize my employee just because of small error he made while processing your payment. b.	I can give my personal address if you don’t trust my word. c.	I will transfer money from my account to you if your cashback is not processed in a day or two. d.	I don’t know what salesperson committed but as per my company SOP we cannot deliver your work as per your expectations Vaishakh: He was a project/ operations manager, joined this project as a replacement to Suraj. He was trying to help me to achieve the timelines to the best of his capacity. However, he got stuck in the middle of Boito’s complicated SOPs, lack of production unit at Mumbai and company’snnot so collaborative relationship with Bonito approved vendors.  Dhaval: He was interior designer for my projects. He is gem from your team. He listened to each of my requirement extremely well. He designed the home virtually the way I wanted. He has significantly exceeded my expectations. He went above and beyond to help me on certain operational challenges on the project which was not under his remit. He is responsive, responsible and takes ownership of his work wholeheartedly. Dhaval is also down to earth and very young independent enthusiastic designer Bonito has got. Only his contribution from whole Bonito team was noteworthy.  I am either neutral on my responses or dissatisfied with everything else. Overall, it was not at all a great experience, and I felt like I wasted my money on your company excluding the designer fee.

column25: As a returning customer of your esteemed organization, I had high expectations based on my previous positive experiences. Unfortunately, I encountered significant disappointment and frustration during the middle phase of the recent project. There appeared to be a lack of communication and attentiveness from both the Project Head (PH) and the team members involved.  Given my experience in the IT industry, where effective communication is critical for addressing customer issues and ensuring project success, I was surprised by the lapses in these areas. It felt as though the project was not adequately managed, leaving me feeling unsupported and neglected.  Suggestions for Improvement:  Communication: Effective communication is crucial, especially when customers may not be able to manage every detail. Despite our active involvement in discussions, we often had to provide extensive direction. This lack of proactive input from the project team hindered the overall progress.  Project Management: The final design phase felt rushed, as we were pressured to complete everything hastily before moving to the QC phase. Additionally, there was a noticeable lack of follow-up and transparency, particularly regarding project updates and staff changes.  Responsiveness: It seemed that the Project Head (PH) was primarily responsive when financial matters were involved but less so when addressing project issues or customer concerns.  To improve future projects, I recommend ensuring consistent and proactive communication, especially in the event of delays or changes. Regular updates and proper tracking are essential for keeping the project on track and maintaining customer satisfaction.  Overall Rating: My rating reflects primarily related to the execution and design phases. If the issues highlighted in other phases are addressed in future projects, I believe the overall experience can be significantly improved.  I hope these observations will be taken into account to enhance future projects and better meet customer expectations.

column25: I'd like to share a few thoughts that might help enhance the client experience for future projects:  Comprehensive Checklist: It would be very helpful to have a detailed checklist that covers all possible options to be discussed with the client for each room — including furniture, paint, lighting fixtures, storage solutions, soft furnishings, and other design elements. Not every client has done extensive research, and for many, this may be the only time they engage in a professional interior design project. A guided and thorough approach would go a long way in ensuring clarity and satisfaction.  Face-to-Face Discussions: Regular, timely in-person meetings to address key decisions and major issues can make the process much smoother. It ensures that the vision stays aligned and helps avoid confusion down the line.  Dedicated Point of Contact: When multiple people handle the same project, it often leads to miscommunication and inconsistent information, making it difficult for clients to keep track of what was discussed and what gets executed. Having one dedicated person from start to finish would make the experience more seamless and efficient.  I genuinely believe that your team has the potential to go above and beyond in delivering exceptional service, and these suggestions could help elevate the overall journey for clients like us.

There is a need to enhance communication and coordination within your internal team. It often seems that the previous team is disconnected and sidelined during the transition to the new team, with little to no ongoing involvement. Specifically, teams as Sales, Design, Project, and On-Site Execution tend to operate independently, making it difficult to identify a single point of contact who can reliably provide updates or address my concerns and queries.  Based on my experience, working with your Design Team has been challenging and has contributed to multiple delays in project execution. However, I must compliment your On-site Execution Team, as their professionalism and approach were completely different and more positive compared to the impressions given by your Design Team.  Additionally, there is a need to review your internal processes and the time taken to handle projects in a more simplified manner. I have observed that your team struggles to effectively explain even technical and commercial queries I have raised. Surprisingly, delays caused by these issues have led to suggestions by your team of scope degression and external addressing, which could be avoided with better internal coordination and clarity.  In short, as a result of these issues, I have unfortunately faced shortcomings and compromises in the quality of my home interior work, primarily due to mistakes made by your Designer or Technical Team, which I consider a shortcoming on your part.

1. Requirement Capture & Design Phase * Requirements were clearly communicated during early discussions; however, not all were incorporated into the final design dockets.  * For example, the mandir pull-out shelf requirement (shared on 27 September 2025 via WhatsApp) was not included in the design, leading to later disputes and rework.  * Similarly, design intent inconsistencies (e.g., membrane vs carcass finish) indicate gaps in translating agreed requirements into execution.  This reflects a deficiency in requirement capture and design validation.  2. Design-to-Execution Gaps * There were multiple instances where execution did not align with approved design intent, including:     * Incorrect sizing of the water purifier cabinet      * Finish inconsistencies in cabinetry      * Alignment issues in kitchen installations  These issues required corrective actions post-execution, resulting in avoidable delay and inconvenience.  3. Measurement & Planning Errors * The incorrect sizing of the water purifier cabinet led to:     * Cabinet removal and rework      * Reinstallation of gas meter and pipeline      * Additional out-of-pocket expenses  Such errors indicate lack of adequate measurement validation prior to execution and constitute a clear service lapse.  4. Execution Quality & Rectifications * Several elements required rework and rectification, including:     * Hob–chimney alignment      * Moulding alignment      * Finishing inconsistencies  * Even post-completion, touch-up gaps and paint stains (window grill, balcony floor) remain.  These indicate inconsistency in quality control and execution supervision.  5. Coordination & Communication Gaps * There were repeated instances of:     * Delayed responses      * Missed timelines      * Need for continuous follow-ups  This impacted overall project efficiency and added to the execution delay.  6. Timeline Deviation & Impact * The project experienced significant delay beyond the originally committed timeline (September completion).  * As a direct consequence, I have incurred:     * Additional rental outflow      * Associated costs and logistical inconvenience  This establishes a clear financial impact attributable to project delay.  7. Financial Handling & Reimbursements * Out-of-pocket expenses incurred due to execution errors (e.g., gas line reinstallation, additional materials, civil work) are pending reimbursement.  * Attempts to convert such reimbursements into store credit instead of cash settlement are not appropriate, as these are actual expenses incurred.   8. Compensation Framework * While standard delay compensation as per agreement has been referenced, it does not adequately reflect:     * The extent of delay      * The actual financial impact incurred      * The additional coordination and inconvenience caused   9. Closure Process * There have been attempts to proceed with project closure and warranty release while:     * Rectifications were pending      * Financial settlements were incomplete  Closure should ideally follow complete execution and settlement, not precede it.  Overall Position While the physical scope of work has now largely been completed after multiple iterations, the overall experience reflects: * Gaps in requirement capture  * Execution and measurement deficiencies  * Delays beyond committed timelines  * Financial impact due to such delays  This feedback is being shared to ensure an accurate record of the project experience and its implications.  I remain open to resolving the pending financial aspects, including reimbursement and delay-related compensation, in a fair and reasonable manner. I trust the above will be taken constructively and addressed appropriately. Regards, Savio Sequeira
